In the present book, we quickly discover that the "Spanish priest" is actually Jacques Collin, alias "Vautrin," a master criminal first introduced to readers in Balzac's Father Goriot (1835). Lucien develops a relationship with Esther van Gobseck, a prostitute (the "courtesan" of the title). With these three main figures — Lucien, Vautrin, and Esther — Balzac explores the corruption of the aristocracy, the world of prostitution, the courts, and the prisons of 19th-century Paris. With masterful depictions of society and individual psychology, Balzac is considered a father of realism in fiction. - Summary by Bruce Pirie The Balzac Lost Illusions trilogy preceding this book: Lost Illusions: Two Poets Lost Illusions: A Distinguished Provincial at Paris Lost Illusions: Ève and David

title: The Parasocial Paradox | The risks & realities of being visible online
author: Unpublic
description:
There's the public side of being a creator - the growth, the audience, the brand deals, the sales. And then there's the side nobody talks about: the risks, the exposure, the realities that come with making a livelihood out of being visible online.

As a professional creator, you're business is built on the relationships you have with your audience, the ones you need to grow, to monetize, and to actually serve them well.

But along with all the upsides of building an audience come risks. Risks that nobody tells you about, often until you find yourself staring them down face-to-face. Privacy and security exposure. Mental health and burnout. Platform dependency. Income volatility. Public call outs. Cancellation. AI cloning your content without consent. Audiences that feel a little too close for comfort. 

And the worst part: There's no safety net and no playbook for any of it. Our goal is to change that. 

In each episode of the Parasocial Paradox, you'll hear experienced creators dig into the tensions, risk, and realities of  being visible online platform risk, financial instability, AI, physical safety, mental health, audience dynamics, all of it. There's a lot of upside, and there's a lot that can go wrong, and we don't talk about the latter nearly enough.

Think of it as listening in on a private creator mastermind or hallway track at a conference, where nobody's performing and nothing's off the table.

Because while these conversations are already happening, they’re happening behind closed doors in 1:1 calls, masterminds, and group chats. 

If we want more creators to be less exposed, more secure, and more able to build successful, sustainable careers, we need to put that stuff on the mic, because the truth of this business is this: it's wonderful, and it’s also really hard.

Hosted by Susan Boles, who spent years in federal law enforcement and security before becoming a creator herself. After a decade of watching creators build with zero infrastructure for protection, she started Unpublic, a creator safety and privacy service built for people whose livelihood depends on showing up online.

The risks are real. So are the rewards. And it's time we actually talked about both. That's the paradox every creator feels - building relationships with an audience while navigating the real risks of being visible online.

Pull up a chair, grab your coffee, and turn off the ring light. We're not performing here.

Every week, your host Joana Beldade, sits down for one honest conversation about what it actually takes to build a business that works for your life. Not someone else's life. Yours.

Jo has spent 8 years working with service-based entrepreneurs across Portugal and North America, helping them repackage their offers, raise their prices, build scalable systems, and stop trading hours for money. She's also a certified yoga teacher, a former Air Force officer, has a landscape architect master degree, and is a single mum to a five-year-old boy. She launched a programme from a hospital bed at 29 weeks pregnant, hit rock bottom with thirteen euros in her bank account, and rebuilt. Slowly. Quietly. Without anyone watching.

That's what this podcast is about.

Building quietly. Without the performance. Without the promise that it'll be easy or fast or linear. With the honest acknowledgment that life happens, that hard seasons are real, and that it's still possible to build something meaningful on the other side of them.

No guests who say everything is possible if you just hustle harder. No advice that ignores the human being behind the business. No six-figure promises.

Just real talk. From someone who has been in the trenches, made the mistakes, and kept going anyway.

This manuscript tells of a man, Severin von Kusiemski, so infatuated with a woman, Wanda von Dunajew, that he requests to be treated as her slave, and encourages her to treat him in progressively more degrading ways. At first Wanda does not understand or relate to the request, but after humouring Severin a bit she finds the advantages of the method to be interesting and enthusiastically embraces the idea; though at the same time, she disdains Severin for allowing her to do so.

title: Limited Company Property Finance
author: Limited Company Property Finance
description:
Limited Company Property Finance
The UK podcast on how landlords fund property inside a limited company. Hosted by Georgina, every episode explains how lenders price and underwrite a company or SPV buy-to-let mortgage, on the structure, the interest cover ratio and the personal guarantee, and walks through the finance behind buying, incorporating, remortgaging, HMOs, bridging and scaling a property company. Practical, current, and grounded in real market data.

A company buy-to-let mortgage is not a personal mortgage. It is a loan to a special purpose vehicle, an SPV registered under SIC code 68100 or 68209, that exists only to hold rental property. The company borrows, the rent services the loan, and the directors give personal guarantees. That structure is the answer to Section 24, which removed full mortgage interest relief for higher-rate personal landlords, and it is why a record share of buy-to-let is now bought through a company. Every episode of Limited Company Property Finance translates that into plain English.

This is the show for the people who own, build, buy, invest in or advise on UK rental property through a company: investors, portfolio landlords and the accountants and solicitors around them. We cover the full range of limited company property finance and SPV mortgages, from core company buy-to-let and remortgages, to transferring an existing portfolio into a company, HMO and multi-unit finance, SPV bridging and refurbishment, and portfolio and group facilities. We explain typical loan to value, the 125% interest cover test versus 145% personally, the small rate premium, and how to match each requirement to the right lender.

We name the specialist SPV lenders, the likes of Paragon, Kent Reliance, Fleet Mortgages, Foundation Home Loans, Landbay and Precise, and we explain why the intermediary-only names, such as The Mortgage Works, Leeds and Coventry, matter: you cannot approach them directly, only through a broker with whole-of-market access across a panel of more than 100 lenders. We also explain how the Bank of England base rate feeds through to company borrowing.

Every figure is grounded in current data. We draw on Hamptons, Companies House, Paragon, UK Finance and HMRC, so you get credible numbers rather than guesswork: the number of buy-to-let companies, the share of new purchases made through a company, and the lending picture. The aim is simple. Help you read your own company the way a lender reads it, and present it to the right lender on the right terms.

Hosted by Georgina at Limited Company Property Finance, with written analysis by founder Matt Lenzie. New episodes land quarterly, with the occasional bulletin when the rate cycle or the tax picture shifts.

author: Kim Fitzgerald - Coach - Angry Prayers - Restoring Faith for Moms Struggling Through The Hell of It
description:
Have you ever been angry with God? 
Are you walking through a season so painful that you’ve started to wonder if God is angry with you?
Have you been praying and praying but starting to wonder if God is even listening?
Have you put off praying because you just don’t know what to say?
If you answered yes to any of these questions, this podcast is for you.
Angry Prayers is a podcast for hurting moms navigating grief, loss, heartbreak, disappointment, unanswered prayers, and life’s hardest seasons.... also known as the hell of it.
It’s for the mom whose faith feels shaken, whose heart feels broken, and whose prayers are messy.
Together, we’ll have honest conversations about what it means to trust God when life doesn’t make sense. We’ll talk about pain, anger, grief, healing, hope, and the kind of faith that survives even when everything around you feels like it’s falling apart.
Each episode will help you:
• Strengthen your faith during difficult seasons
• Find hope when God feels distant
• Bring your honest emotions to God without guilt or shame
• Discover that questioning God doesn’t disqualify you from His love
• Develop healing faith in the middle of life’s hardest moments
Why should you listen?
Because I’ve lived it.
I’ve experienced devastating loss, heartbreak, disappointment, and seasons that left me asking questions I never thought I’d ask. I’ve cried out to God through tears, confusion, frustration, and anger. I’ve wrestled with my faith while desperately trying to hold on to it at the same time.
What I’ve learned is that God is not afraid of our questions. He is not intimidated by our doubts. And He doesn’t stop loving us when our prayers are messy, or even filled with anger.
If you’re looking for healing faith in the middle of a hard season, I’d love for you to join me.
Subscribe to Angry Prayers and join our private Facebook community where hurting moms can find encouragement, support, prayer, and a safe place to walk through hard seasons together.
You don’t have to carry this alone.

description:

Acres of Diamonds by Russell H. Conwell is a classic motivational book based on his famous lecture, which argues that opportunities for success and wealth are abundant in one's own community and circumstances, not far away. The central theme, illustrated by the parable of a man who sells his farm to search for diamonds only to have a mine discovered on his old land, is that "dig in your own backyard". Conwell, a minister and founder of Temple University, used the story to teach that true fortune comes from recognizing and acting on the potential in one's immediate environment, emphasizing that success is a spiritual idea built on character, initiative, and helping others.

This podcast is for you.

It is.

I am Natalia. I grew up in Kazakhstan, studied in Russia, and have lived and worked as an educator in China, Turkey, Czechia, and Thailand. I was not born with a Western passport or a perfect plan. I left with a scholarship, a suitcase, and a quiet truth inside me that said my future was somewhere beyond the borders I knew.

That decision changed everything.

In this podcast I share real stories, honest conversations, and practical guidance for women who are standing at that same crossroads right now — wondering if they are good enough, brave enough, and ready enough to take that first step into an international teaching life.

You are. And this show will prove it.

title: Caring For your Garden
description:
Choose and care for fragrant flowers: pick plants with thick, colorful petals, plant companions and stagger bloom times to extend scent, keep consistent watering, and ensure good air circulation rather than hot, dry, or stagnant humidity.
– Avoid planting fragrant varieties in areas with poor ventilation or extreme dryness.
Beginner garden setup: assess the site, remove weeds, improve soil with compost or manure, start with annuals, and read plant labels for light and water needs.
– Basic tools: hose, pruning shears, digging spade, shovel, weeder, garden knife.
Household remedies (use with caution): reuse banana peels, coffee grounds, and tea leaves as organic nutrients; olive oil + sugar removes dirt from hands; castor/mineral oil can shine indoor leaves.
– Do not use table salt on plants; it can harm or kill them.
Common garden problems and fixes: control weeds with targeted removal or herbicide, remove paving weeds with careful spot treatments, water lawns in the morning to reduce fungal risk, and treat yellow leaves for nutrient deficiency or sap-sucking pests.
– Inspect plants for insects before applying oil sprays or pesticides.
Dog-friendly garden strategies: train or leash puppies to one designated potty area (wood chips or gravel), clean feces daily, redirect digging with barriers and supervised play, and provide alternative digging/play zones.
Plant-specific care basics: shrubs need little water once established and regular pruning; annuals require frequent watering and fertilizing; roses need ~6 hours sun and regular morning watering; vegetables need spacing and row planting.
Seasonal care overview: in fall prune, clear leaves, till, mulch perennials, and plant bulbs; in spring plant annuals/perennials and fertilize; in summer maintain, weed, water more, and fertilize twice monthly; in winter protect winter crops with mulch, compost leaves, and store/clean tools.

title: Simple Hidden Road To Success
description:
Success is often seen as a destination reached through luck, talent, or special opportunities. However, the true road to success is usually much simpler and often hidden from plain sight. It is built on small daily habits, discipline, consistency, and the willingness to keep moving forward even when progress seems slow.

Many successful people did not achieve their goals overnight. They focused on learning, improving their skills, staying positive, and refusing to quit when challenges appeared. The hidden road to success involves setting clear goals, managing time wisely, learning from failures, and maintaining a strong work ethic.

Every small step taken today contributes to future achievements. Success is not about perfection; it is about persistence. By remaining committed to your vision and taking action every day, you can unlock opportunities and create a path toward lasting success.

Remember, the hidden road to success is not a secret formula—it is the simple practice of doing the right things consistently over time.

description:
I believe our experiences shape us for a reason. We all carry unique stories and trauma, and healing begins when we’re willing to feel it and genuinely want to do and feel and show up better.

I share my experiences honestly, openly, and without filter. Some conversations will be light, others will be uncomfortable. I plan to explore vulnerable topics, difficult truths, and some of the darker places I’ve seen and lived through, because healing doesn’t happen by avoiding what hurts, it happens when we’re willing to look at it.
Even though our trauma is unique to each of us, I want this to be a space where people feel less alone in their experiences. A place where difficult conversations aren't met with judgment, shame, or fear. Because maybe what I’ve been through, you’ve been through too.
You never have to share your story. But my hope is that something in these conversations helps you feel seen, valued, heard, understood, and a little less alone whatever it is your going through or have overcome.
If this resonates, great. If it doesn’t, that’s okay too.
Nuances with Nura exists to explore the gray areas of human behaviour, challenge limiting perspectives, and encourage growth through awareness rather than victimhood.

title: Video BOOK: ADHD: an insider View, from Dr. Richard Murdoch Montgomery, MD, PhD and....ADHD Patient
author: Richard Murdoch Montgomery
description:
The book is "ADHD: Disorder, Difference, Spectrum, and Human Adaptation" (2026), published by the Scottish Science Society Press. It is a monumental 51-chapter masterpiece written by Dr. Richard Murdoch Montgomery, MD, PhD, who is a clinical neurologist, a mathematician, and a carrier of the ADHD trait himself.

Because Dr. Montgomery rejects simple checklists and popular "pop-psychology" simplifications in favor of deep mathematical modeling, evolutionary biology, and rich narrative prose, a video podcast adapting this book chapter by chapter must be equally sophisticated.

Here is a production blueprint for a high-end, multi-season video podcast series that directly adapts the real structure and scientific depth of Dr. Montgomery’s text.


  • The Aesthetic: A sleek "academic laboratory meets late-night conversation" aesthetic. To honor Dr. Montgomery's note that his book is best read like "a long conversation, of the sort one has by candlelight," the lighting should be intimate and cinematic, contrasted against interactive digital light-boards used to sketch out neurological paths and mathematical models.
  • The Anti-Reductionist Rule: In alignment with Dr. Montgomery’s explicit resistance to the contemporary medical habit of reducing everything to perfunctory summaries or bulleted lists, the podcast episodes will favor deep, continuous, storytelling-driven exploration of the data rather than superficial rapid-fire segments.


Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Permission to Love Yourself
author: Jolanna Torres
description:
Are you a mom who feels like you’re not allowed to take time for yourself? Do you find yourself stuck in toxic relationships, friendships, or situationships just because you don’t want to hurt anyone's feelings? And when you finally escape, does the exact same cycle repeat itself with a different person?

If saying "no" feels like a curse word, you are not alone. Maybe you desperately want to please the people you love—doing things they didn't even ask for out of the kindness of your heart—only to feel upset when they aren't grateful. Or maybe you struggle to see your own value, accepting a life of purely serving others while secretly knowing deep down that something is missing.

Welcome to Permission to Love Yourself—the safe space where you can hang up your superwoman cape and just be a woman. Not a mom, a wife, or a CEO. Just you.

As a Self-Love and Confidence Coach, I guide women on their journey toward loving the woman God created them to be. I know exactly how you feel because I lived it. I went from being terrified of being alone, battling chronic people-pleasing, and fearing I'd only ever amount to throwing boxes for a living, to falling in love with my solitude, setting boundaries, and discovering my true purpose.

This podcast is your invitation to dig deep into who you are. Follow along to learn how to pour into yourself without the mom guilt, master the art of saying "no," find your authenticity, and confidently walk away from toxic situations.

title: Track News Daily | Daily Track & Field Podcast

description:
Track News Daily is your go-to daily podcast for the latest track and field news, NCAA track coverage, sprint rivalries, marathon headlines, world records, and everything happening across the sport. Hosted by Serenity Douglas, the show delivers daily analysis, reactions, breaking stories, athlete updates, race previews, and in-depth discussions covering the biggest moments in track & field.

From Olympic stars and NCAA standouts to World Athletics drama and viral performances, Track News Daily keeps fans connected to the stories shaping the future of the sport. Whether you follow sprinting, distance running, hurdles, relays, or major championships, this podcast brings nonstop updates and commentary every single day.

Subscribe now for daily track & field coverage, expert reactions, and the latest news from around the running world.

title: Confidence Without Pretending
author: Jessica Giuffre
description:
Hosted by coach and EMDR practitioner Jessica Giuffré, Confidence Without Pretending explores what it really means to feel comfortable in your own skin.
Across this series, Jessica shares reflections, practical tools, nervous-system-informed insights, and fresh perspectives on confidence, self-worth, relationships, comparison, people-pleasing, and the stories we tell ourselves.
Rather than teaching you how to appear confident, these episodes explore how confidence develops naturally when you build trust in yourself.
Learn more at BlueEmeraldCoaching.com.

title: The Practical People Manager with Anna Thomas
author: Anna Thomas
description:
The Practical People Manager is the podcast for line managers, HR professionals, and business leaders who want to manage people well, with more confidence and less jargon.
Hosted by HR Expert, Anna Thomas, each episode brings real conversations with experts and practitioners covering the topics that matter most at work, from mental health and neurodiversity to menopause and beyond.
This is practical, people-first leadership. No fluff. Just honest conversations to help you listen better, lead with confidence, and support the people around you.

description: The Wise Unicorn Podcast explores intuition, healing, spirituality, relationships, creativity, and the deeper layers of the human experience through honest, grounded conversation. Hosted by Lauren Noelle Salgado a psychic medium, breathwork facilitator, and yoga teacher. Each episode blends mysticism with real life, offering thoughtful conversations, personal insight, and transformative perspectives for people seeking deeper meaning, clarity, and connection.

description:
Welcome to The Beauty Blueprint! 💄✨
I’m CeCe, founder of CeCe’s Beauty Bar and a bridal makeup artist passionate about creating timeless looks and inspiring confidence. The Beauty Blueprint is a video podcast where beauty meets business, offering expert advice, tips, and industry insights.
Here, we’ll explore:
💋 Makeup Trends – Stay ahead with the latest looks.
💍 Bridal Tips – From flawless wedding day makeup to skincare prep.
🌟 Everyday Hacks – Quick beauty tips to elevate your routine.
🎙️ Industry Insights – Conversations with photographers, models, designers, and more.

title: The Breakfast Ball: a daily golf podcast
author: breakfastballdailygolfpodcast
description: The Breakfast Ball is a daily news and analysis podcast brought to you by golf journalists and authors, Shane Ryan and Evin Priest. It’s extremely serious – and historic, having been created in 1934.

title: The Calvis Wyant Podcast
author: Calvis Wyant Luxury Homes
description:
Join Tony Calvis, Jr. from Calvis Wyant Luxury Homes as he uncovers the experience of design-building luxury custom homes across Scottsdale, Paradise Valley, and the Phoenix-Metro Area in Arizona.
Meet with the architects, interior designers, and the trades you'll work with when building a Calvis Wyant home as we take you into their offices and showrooms around the Valley. If you're considering a project with a luxury custom homebuilder, this is the place to start.

title: YGN Radio
author: YGNRadio
description:
YGN Radio is a biweekly collection of audio essays about anime and manga — examining the themes, motifs, and hidden visual language of Japanese animation through the lens of Yugen: the profound and mysterious sense of beauty in the universe. The feeling beneath the feeling. What something is saying without actually saying it. 
Each episode goes beyond plot summary and fan discussion to ask the deeper questions: what is this show actually saying? What does this recurring image mean? Why does this specific moment in animation history matter? And why did it hit you the way it did?

title: Off The Shelf With Sarah
author: Sarah Kearney
description: Cozy Conversations with Authors

title: Black Spots
author: Gareth Horrocks
description:
This podcast is a warning. A call to arms against a movement that loves humanity so much they would destroy everything that makes us human. 
We’re living through a very different kind of pandemic. This new virus is not spread through infected particles but “Black Spots”. People have found them placed in their mail, on their windshield, or in their pocket after their commute. Have you been slipped a “Black Spot”? 
The cult responsible for spreading this curse call themselves the “Original Position”. This podcast will explain their origins and why they must be stopped.

title: Two Mics No Masking
author: Autistic Culture Podcast Network
description:
Two Mics, No Masking is a podcast on the Autistic Culture Podcast Network about what it really means to be neurodivergent at home, at work, and in a world that often misunderstands how our brains work.


Hosted by Katie Scott (founder of THE COLLECTIVE) and Jenna Goldstein (founder of ND3), best friends, late-identified autistic women, and parents to neurodivergent children, the show explores neurodivergence with honesty, humour, and depth. From parenting and relationships to burnout, identity, and the systems we’re constantly navigating, each episode digs beneath the surface to name what’s actually happening, without fixing, performing, or pretending.


Produced for the Autistic Culture Podcast Network.


🎧 Two Mics, No Masking is available on Spotify, Apple Podcasts, and all major platforms.

🌈 Celebrate autistic voices with early access, ad-free listening, and our full archive at AutisticCulturePlus.com

🌐 Visit www.autisticculturepodcastnetwork.com

📲 Follow us on Instagram: @autisticculturepodcast

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: A Day In Beijing with Chen
author: Chen
description: You think you know China. You probably don't. This is a Beijing-inspired travel podcast that exists to break cultural stereotypes of China. It digs into Chinese history, traditions, and artistry from the people who make Beijing extraordinary. Not AI-produced, not a one-trip vlogger, not a TikTok trend. We curate conscious travel experiences, exclusively backing independent local businesses and putting money back into the community. Not a slogan but how we operate. I'm Chen, a Chinese law graduate, an LSE anthropologist, and a Beijinger who's sick of leaving others to explain my hometown.

We cover agronomy strategies, fertilizer market updates, and the decisions that impact your bottom line. Hear from industry experts and local farmers as they share what’s working, what’s changing, and how they’re navigating agriculture today. 
Stay informed, stay connected, and stay ahead with United Prairie.

With nearly ten years at the helm of a successful 7-figure agency, Seth shares his wealth of knowledge and experience in building and scaling a digital marketing powerhouse. The podcast features enlightening interviews with other top agency executives, offering a treasure trove of strategies, tips, and insights. Whether you're an aspiring entrepreneur or a seasoned professional, this podcast is your essential guide to mastering the local agency landscape.

This podcast, hostet by Sara, is in Service of the New Earth embodied human. Each episode brings about spiritual realization and soul-body awakening; Held in the Eternal Presence of God. In this interview series, Larissa Aries sits down with electronic music artists from around the world to put into words what they've already been telling us through their music. Every artist has a unique story to tell. She dives into the stories of people who create, connect, and find themselves in sound, community, and the dancefloor.

Got a dilemma? Stuck in a situation or sent a text that you shouldn’t have? (We all have there’s no judgement) Submit it here and we’ll work it out together. You’re welcome to stay completely anonymous or add whatever info you feel comfortable.


I’m not a therapist or life coach or anyone that is remotely qualified to fix your life. But I am someone who enjoys untangling a sticky situation. After all a problem shared is a problem halved, or doubled. Think of me as the friend you text when something confusing, chaotic or mildly disastrous happens.

Based on my personal experience, it is not therapy, advice or a substitute for professional support when that's needed. It simply puts words to things that can be hard to name.

title: The 10-Minute Rundown
description: Caught up in ten minutes. Every single day.The 10-Minute Rundown does the impossible part of staying informed: it reads everything so you don't have to. Each morning it sweeps thousands of stories from the world's major news outlets — AP, Reuters, BBC, NYT, NPR, Guardian and more — and pulls the key takeaways from the best news podcasts, then transcribes, de-duplicates, and summarizes it into one tight, fast briefing.One show. Eight beats — world news, AI, crypto, cybersecurity, space, politics, tech, and sports — plus the sharpest insights from the podcasts everyone's talking about. No repeated stories day to day, no filler, no doomscrolling. Just the signal, read by your two AI co-anchors, in about ten minutes.Hit play with your coffee and start the day already informed.

It features interviews with The Sell Well Agency clients and successful case studies, plus behind-the-scenes breakdowns. description:
Join Texas Lieutenant Governor Dan Patrick for candid conversations about the issues shaping Texas and America. Each week, Lt. Dan goes beyond the headlines to discuss politics, policy, culture, and current events with elected officials, business leaders, policy experts, and the people directly involved in the stories that matter most.
From the Texas Capitol to Washington, D.C., you'll get insight, context, and perspective often missing from the mainstream media. Whether it's legislation, elections, economic issues, or the cultural debates defining our future, Lt. Dan delivers straightforward analysis and honest discussion without the spin.
Because understanding what happens next starts with understanding what's really happening now.

title: Core of the Universe (Audiobook)

description:
Sex and intimacy are central to our lives but conflicting thoughts and powerful feelings often get in the way of lasting joy. 
While many people find it difficult to talk about this subject, Rev. Sun Myung Moon boldly stated that sex is sacred and at the very core of the universe. He and his wife, Dr. Hak Ja Han Moon, spent a lifetime teaching how our sexual organs are God’s most precious gift to us, and how individuals can build beautiful marriages filled with deep, life-transforming intimacy. 
Core of the Universe invites you to examine what you want for your own lives.

He explained that true spirituality should be in daily practice, inner maturity, and living truth in everyday life. Soul science, reality creation, and higher consciousness are explained in a clear and practical way.
The law of karma is explained as a beautiful system of learning, responsibility, and conscious evolution. Master Pradeep Vijay is speaking on the art of manifestation and spiritual awareness.

title: The Data Shaman Podcast

Numbers in mind, roots deep in the ground. Seeking the truth wherever it can be found.


Welcome to The Data Shaman, a self-help podcast for the analytical thinkers, the seekers, and anyone trying to make sense of the human experience.

We look past the coffee-stained scrolls of standard self-improvement advice to find what actually works. Blending hard logic with deep mindfulness, we explore the science and soul of personal growth. If you're ready to master your habits, conquer your mindset, and take full control of your life's trajectory, you're in the right place.

Each episode brings researchers, specialists, and FMT pioneers into the conversations clinicians actually need — dysbiosis, chronic GI, allergies, oncology, and root-cause medicine you can use Monday morning. Hosted by independent travel designer John Redding, this series opens up the world of travel through conversations with the people who shape it; from visionary suppliers crafting remarkable experiences to the tourism authorities bringing destinations to life.
Each episode offers candid insights, behind‑the‑scenes stories, and a deeper look at what makes certain journeys stand out. Together, we explore how these experts inspire travellers, what sets their experiences apart.

title: The Newsman Podcast
author: Travis Boles
description:
The Newsman Podcast is about the people who lived the stories — journalists, filmmakers, storytellers, and the fascinating professionals who shaped the world one deadline, one frame, and one decision at a time.


Hosted by Emmy-nominated filmmaker Travis Boles, the show is inspired by his father Kenny Boles — a 47-year broadcast news veteran who started at 17 at KTRE in Lufkin, Texas, rose to Managing Editor at KNBC-TV Los Angeles covering the O.J. Simpson trial, the Rodney King riots, and the Michael Jackson investigation, and came home to East Texas to spend 22 years building the next generation of journalists as News Director at KLTV. He was inducted into the Lone Star Emmy Silver Circle for lifetime achievement in 2016. He passed away in 2018. 


New episodes every two weeks.

title: unstuck, by Nicole

Life, leadership, business and relationships have a way of leaving us feeling stuck. Stuck in old patterns, limiting beliefs, difficult decisions and ways of thinking that no longer serve us.
Each week, executive coach and human performance strategist Nicole Maro shares practical insights, fresh perspectives and honest conversations to help you think differently, lead differently and move forward with greater clarity, confidence and purpose.
Because sometimes the smallest shift in perspective can change everything.

He specializes in sexual addiction and trauma recovery and has personally overcome these challenges. He is passionate about helping others find hope and healing in their own journey.

author: Andre Franklin
description:
Welcome to The Berean Podcast! We believe that the truth isn’t found in popular opinion, social trends, or religious trends—it’s found in the pages of scripture. We take a “Berean” approach: we examine the evidence, we wrestle with the hard questions, and we let the Bible speak for itself.

Whether you are a long-time believer, a skeptic, or simply searching for answers, you’re invited to sit at the table with me. Join me as we examine the scriptures daily to see if these things are so.

title: Below the Waterline: The Leadership work no one talks about.
author: Dori Etter
description:
You've done everything you were told to do to get freedom as a Founder. And you're still the bottleneck. 
Below the Waterline is the weekly show for growth-stage founders who can't figure out why the fixes never hold. Each week, Dori Etter goes past the systems structure most consultants stop at, into the identity, beliefs, and control patterns that quietly run your company. 
Operations and identity, above and below the waterline, working together. 
This is the leadership work that produces real change - but no one else talks about. 
New episodes weekly.

title: AROUND THE TABLE
author: The Porch Missional Community
description:
Around The Table is a discipleship podcast from The Porch, sharing teachings and conversations from our Bible studies and house church gatherings.


These episodes are less about polished sermons and more about faithful formation—Scripture opened together, lives shaped in community, and hearts anchored in Jesus.


Come listen, slow down, and build on what lasts.

title: Relationship Reset: Reignite, Reconnect, Rebuild
author: Katie Rössler
description:
Feeling stuck in your relationship after years together? Relationship Reset is your go-to podcast for busy, high-achieving couples ready to break free from autopilot and rebuild a thriving partnership. Join relationship expert Katie Rössler, LPC for practical tools, real-life stories, and actionable advice to reignite passion, rebuild trust, and reconnect on a deeper level. Whether you’re navigating communication breakdowns, struggling with intimacy, or just feel disconnected, this podcast is here to help you transform your relationship—and create the love you’ve always envisioned.

Perfect for couples who want to reignite their spark and reconnect with purpose. It’s never too late to hit reset.

title: Built From Scratch
author: Sagar Pratap Singh
description:
Stories, reflections, and lessons from work, business, caregiving, loss, growth, and leadership.
For anyone who is trying to build - from scratch - one day at a time.

title: Baseball from the 513 to the 619
author: Jack
description: All things baseball with one Cincinnati Reds fan and one San Diego Padres fan.  Stats, prospect evaluations, and team outlooks throughout the season.

title: Arts For All Network Audio Files
author: Sophie Macdonald
description:
A collection of audio files from Arts For All Network workshops, webinars, hui and artist Q&As.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Level-Up with Yolanda Charles
author: Yolanda Charles
description:
Welcome to LEVEL-UP with Yolanda Charles, the podcast where growth meets purpose and the next version of you starts here.
I'm your host, Yolanda Charles—entrepreneur, paramedic, future nurse, mother of three, and someone who believes that no matter where you are in life, there's always another level waiting for you.
Each week, we'll have real conversations about leadership, business, entrepreneurship, personal growth, health, financial empowerment, motherhood, and purpose-driven success.
If you're ready to stop settling and start growing, you're in the right place.
Let's level up.

title: Like We Were Never Kids
author: Raye Williams
description:
I spent a lot of years trying to grow up.
Trying to move on. Trying to reinvent myself. Trying to become the version of me that had it all figured out.
But the older I got, the more I realized the stories I was trying hardest to outgrow were the ones still shaping everything.
Like We Were Never Kids is a narrative podcast and original music series where I tell those stories out loud.
Across four decades, twenty episodes, and twenty original songs, I’m going back through childhood, grief, family, trauma, ambition, caregiving, first dreams, big losses, Nashville years, Michigan years, and all the moments that made me who I am — whether I wanted them to or not.
Some episodes are heavy. Some are funny. Some are uncomfortable. Most are all three.
Along the way, you’ll meet my family, hear the people who helped raise me, sit in basement conversations, listen to original songs written for each story, and hopefully remember pieces of your own life too.
Because this isn’t really about nostalgia.
It’s about recognition.
And maybe if we stop pretending we were never kids, we can finally understand the adults we became.
I’ll go first.
Learn more at www.rayewilliams.com

title: The Universal Laws Code
author: Stacey Chillemi
description:
Most people think they're stuck because of circumstances. They're not. They're stuck because of laws they don't know exist.

Monica Ion — founder of the School of Universal Laws, USA Today bestselling author, and Romania's #1 coach — spent 15,000 hours testing a single question: what if the same laws governing physics and mathematics also govern how the human mind creates (or blocks) results? The answer became a framework that makes breakthroughs predictable. In this series, Monica breaks down the 7 Universal Laws and shows exactly how to apply them to business, relationships, patterns, and the decisions that keep people repeating the same outcomes. 

This is for entrepreneurs, leaders, and women who are done with advice that doesn't stick.

title: Backseat Boardroom
author: Kellie
description:
Backseat Boardroom is where real life meets real leadership—no suits, no scripts, no pretending.
Hosted by a former executive, this podcast dives into raw conversations, unexpected lessons, and the truth behind growth, identity, and imposter syndrome. No corporate fluff—just honest, unfiltered insights from everyday moments and the people you were meant to meet.
Because the best leadership lessons don’t come from the boardroom… they come from the backseat.

title: Back-Seat Wrestling
author: The Brothers Smith
description:
Back-Seat Wrestling is a casual wrestling podcast where two lifelong fans share honest, post-show opinions without pretending we’d book it better.
From Attitude Era roots to today’s weekly shows, it’s honest wrestling chat best served after the bell - preferably over a pint.
Back-Seat Wrestling is an independent fan podcast and is not affiliated with or endorsed by WWE or any wrestling promotion. All views and opinions expressed are our own and are intended for discussion and entertainment purposes only.

title: Guava Culture
author: WGC Productions
description: Since its founding in the late 1880s, Tampa Bay has been a vibrant cultural hub sustained by people from all walks of life. Guava Culture features tastemakers of Tampa Bay that make it the glittering jewel of Florida.

title: Connected for Good
author: Loudoun Now
description: Join us for a community conversation celebrating Loudoun County's changemakers, hosted by Claude Moore Charitable Foundation Director of Giving Stephanie Nerantzis and Matt Bender, Director of Community Engagement at the Community Foundation for Loudoun and Northern Fauquier Counties. Made possible through a grant from the Claude Moore Charitable Foundation.

title: RIFF with Tea
author: RIFF with Tea
description:
Welcome to the podcast where HR meets real life, and nothing is off the record.
Whether you’re building boundaries, quitting quietly, or healing loudly, this space is for you.
Expect hot takes, heartfelt moments, and laugh-through-the-chaos energy all with a little HR tea and a lot of truth.

description:

Pigeon Audiobooks brings together the most influential works in history — classics of finance, philosophy, personal development and more — narrated with premium quality and designed to accompany you while you build a stronger and more prosperous life. Timeless, accessible content, created to help you learn at any moment of the day.

author: Time Podcasts
description: The world's most influential business leaders sit down with editor-in-chief Sam Jacobs for TIME's new video interview series, The CEO Moment.

title: Music Industry Daily
author: The Industry Network
description: Music Industry Daily is your daily shot of music industry news — deals, lawsuits, streaming wars, tour drama, artist moves, and global plays. Pour the coffee, hit play, and you'll know what's moving in music before you walk out the door. Weekday mornings, five minutes tops.

title: Inside Sauce
author: Inside Sauce
description:
Inside Sauce is a podcast where casting director Ryan Bernstein and working commercial actor Susan Song pull back the curtain on the world of commercial acting. 
Together, they explore what it really takes to book commercials, from auditions and callbacks to agent relationships, casting decisions, and life on set.
Drawing from both sides of the casting table, Ryan and Susan share insider stories, practical advice, and candid conversations that demystify the commercial acting business. Whether you're just getting started or already booking, they'll help you better understand the industry, sharpen your approach, and navigate your career with confidence.
Along the way, they'll spill the secrets of commercial acting, uncover the ingredients behind successful careers, and get a little saucy with the stories, lessons, and behind-the-scenes insights you won't hear anywhere else.

title: Topping the Curve
author: Sephora Grey
description: Everything you need to know about building a legal career — from someone who has lived every step of it. Weekly episodes covering the law school application process, application strategy, getting A's in law school, landing a job in law school, and planning for a career used to build wealth and financial freedom.

title: Food Menu
description: Join us as we explore the complete Harvester Menu UK 2026, featuring the latest prices, best-selling meals, burgers, rotisserie chicken, BBQ ribs, breakfast favorites, vegan options, desserts, and family meal deals. Discover calorie information, healthy choices, and what makes Harvester one of the UK's most popular family-friendly restaurant chains. Whether you're planning your next visit or simply want to know what's new on the menu, this episode has everything you need to know.

title: Rooted Here
author: The Bamboo Pod
description: Rooted Here, a Bamboo Shoots podcast hosted by Dran Chetty, features deep, one-hour conversations with the leaders, creators, and visionaries shaping our province. This series goes beyond surface-level success to share the raw "human story" of local changemakers—from their early beginnings and the hurdles they overcame, to their current passions and the lasting legacy they want to leave on the community. It is a dedicated space celebrating inspiring growth, resilience, and the people making a genuine difference right where they are.

title: We Are NOT Alone | Aliens, UFOs + More!
author: Mystic Circle
description:
Welcome to We Are NOT Alone | Aliens, UFOs & The Paranormal, your ultimate audio destination for investigations into the unexplained, the supernatural, and the extraterrestrial.Every week, we pull back the veil on the world’s most baffling mysteries. 

From official military UFO/UAP disclosures and historic alien abductions to ancient astronaut theories, occult philosophy, and chilling paranormal encounters, this podcast leaves no stone unturned. We bypass the mainstream media narratives to explore primary sources, declassified government documents, and firsthand accounts of anomalous phenomena.Whether you are fascinated by deep space anomalies, cryptids, interdimensional beings, or the hidden history of secret societies, We Are NOT Alone bridges the gap between meticulous research and the fringe.

Subscribe now on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or your favorite podcast app to join a global community of truth seekers. Hit play, keep an open mind, and remember: the truth isn't just out there, it's right here.

title: Quality Bicycle People
author: Quality Bicycle Products
description: Quality Bicycle People is a podcast from Quality Bicycle Products hosted by Maria Camargo and Mike Riemer, featuring conversations with voices across the bicycle industry—from retailers and athletes to nonprofit leaders and supplier partners. Each episode explores the current and future state of cycling, the ideas shaping the industry, and the personal stories that fuel a passion for riding. It’s a behind-the-scenes look at the people, purpose, and possibilities driving the bike world forward.

title: All Things Well
author: innergetic1h
description:
Because being well isn't one thing, it's all the things.

Welcome to All Things Well, a podcast that explores wellness in all its forms. Hosted by Tamara R. Jackson, each episode features honest conversations about the many ways wellness shows up in our everyday lives, from physical and mental health to relationships, parenting, finances, creativity, faith, grief, purpose, and everything in between.

Through personal stories, expert insights, and lived experiences, All Things Well examines what it means to pursue wholeness in a world that often asks us to compartmentalize our lives. Whether navigating chronic illness, rebuilding after loss, nurturing healthy relationships, caring for family, expressing ourselves through the arts, or finding meaning in life's transitions, this podcast offers practical wisdom and thoughtful reflection for the journey.

This is not a podcast about perfection. It's about presence, growth, resilience, and discovering what wellness looks like in real life.

Join us as we explore all the ways we can live, heal, connect, and flourish, one conversation at a time.

title: Shank House Golf Podcast
author: Shank House Media
description: The Shank House Golf Podcast delivers weekly golf talk and sharp betting analysis. We preview PGA Tour events, break down the odds, and give you actionable picks and insights so you can bet smarter on golf.

Sillage Decision Lab explores organization, mental clarity, and decision-making support for those who want to think more clearly, make more informed decisions, and move forward with greater consistency.
Through practical content, frameworks, and analyses, Sillage Decision Lab offers concrete guidelines to help you better navigate personal and professional complexity.

The Adaptable Strength Podcast is for people navigating life's odd circumstances while still striving to become stronger, healthier, and more capable.
Hosted by strength coach Alex Lefever, each episode explores the real-world challenges that can make progress difficult—from injuries, surgeries, chronic pain, and shift work to mental health struggles, demanding careers, family obligations, and everything in between. Through personal stories, expert insights, and conversations with people who have overcome unique obstacles, the podcast uncovers practical lessons for building strength when life

title: The BK Show - Covering Texas Women’s Sports
author: Brad Kellner - Texas Sports
description:
Your home for Texas Longhorns women’s sports content. Join Brad Kellner (BK), a seasoned Texas insider with more than a decade of professional broadcasting experience, as he breaks down everything you need to know about Texas softball, volleyball, women’s basketball, and more.
From championship runs and transfer portal news to recruiting intel, game analysis, and big-picture conversations, BK brings you passionate, informed coverage of some of the best programs on the Forty Acres.

author: Maple Leaf Adventures
description: Stories of adventures across the coastlines of British Columbia and Alaska. Exploring culture, history and wildlife in beautifully crafted conversations.

title: Ivory Sanctuary
author: Ivory Sanctuary
description:
Ivory Sanctuary was created as a place of comfort, calm and gentle escape. Through immersive sleep stories, guided meditations, and peaceful audio experiences, narrated in Tegan’s soothing voice, you’ll be invited to slow down, let go of the weight you’re carrying and reconnect with a sense of inner peace. 
Whether you’re settling in for sleep, seeking a moment of stillness, or simply needing a safe place to rest, Ivory Sanctuary welcomes you exactly as you are.

title: GTM: Get The Money® [LIVE]
author: Chris Ryan
description:
Hosted by Chris Ryan at SIX, the show explores how revenue acts as a force for good. We’re moving beyond "Go-To-Market" to the real result: Getting the Money to build a future where everyone wins.  We focus on why companies or sales teams stall and how we can help them.

You'll like this show if you like Sangram Vajre and/ or Gary Vaynerchuk.

title: Home School - A Novel
author: Casey Giltner
description:
The debut novel from Casey Giltner. Inspired by classics of the genre - Rosemary's Baby and Carrie. 
Following the sudden, tragic death of her mother, Fourteen-year-old Mira is forced to move across the country and live with her distant aunt. Her Aunt, though warm and loving, is devoutly religious and a strict disciplinarian. She decides to homeschool Mira against her wishes, determined to keep tight control of every aspect of Mira's life. It soon becomes clear that the home school lessons are intended to indoctrinate her into a cult, one that has a particularly sinister obsession with Mira.
A coming-of-age, religious horror-thriller, Home School is a story of youth coming to terms with death, grief, and the mechanisms society and organized religion use to control and shape us.

title: Sermons
author: Christ Alive Church
description: More than a sermon series, Christ Alive is an invitation into community. Each episode brings practical, heartfelt teaching designed to strengthen families, deepen relationships, and root everyday life in faith. Join us as we explore what it means to love God and love each other well — one message, one family, one community at a time.

title: The Midlife Squeeze
author: jackson young
description: No one prepares you for this phase of life.  You're expected to hold it all together — the aging parent who needs more help every month, the kids who need you present, the career that won't pause, and a marriage that can't run on autopilot forever.  And somehow… no one is talking about it.  The Midlife Squeeze is a space for real conversations and proactive planning. We share unfiltered stories from people in the thick of it — because naming the chaos is the first step to navigating it.

title: Who's Better Than Us
author: Rita Gigante
description:
Welcome to Who's Better Than Us, the podcast where nobody holds back and everybody has a story. Join Rita Gigante, daughter of legendary Genovese crime boss Vincent "The Chin" Gigante, psychic medium, healer, and author of The Godfather's Daughter, as she sits down with the people who make up her world. Her wife. Her crew. Fellow Italians. Spiritual healers. Social media personalities. And anyone else who fits into the beautiful chaos that is Rita G.


Expect real conversations, big laughs, Italian American culture, life in the New York/New Jersey area, food, family, spirituality, and the kind of honesty you only get when nobody is performing for anybody.


Who's Better Than Us? Nobody.

title: My Wife's Maiden Voyage
author: Justin & Aine Byrne
description:
Hello and welcome to My Wife's Maiden Voyage.

I'm Justin, and my wife is Aine (on-ya)... I have been a Maiden fanatic for 38 years.. My wife, is not.
Together we talk, laugh and have fun talking about everything Iron Maiden, in the lead up to Iron Maiden's Eddfest Festival in July and beyond.

Listen alone, or with your partner, especially if they are not a fan, so you can relate to both sides of this epic voyage

title: The Good News Show with Andy!
author: Andy Youso
description: Welcome to The Good News Show with Andy — the daily podcast filled with hope, encouragement, laughter, and reminders that God is still working in your story.

title: SharpUnit Daily Market Briefing

description: Sports-market intelligence — Bloomberg for sports betting, not hype. Every weekday, SharpUnit's four analysts break down the day's confirmed model edges: where the model and the market disagree, what sharp money and line movement are signaling, and the risk on every play. Every pick is logged and graded transparently — wins and losses, no cherry-picking. For entertainment only; not betting or financial advice. 21+. If you or someone you know has a gambling problem, call 1-800-GAMBLER.

title: Financial Sentiments
author: Nicholas
description: Financial Sentiments is a podcast about investing, retirement, taxes, estate planning, and the financial decisions that shape real life. Hosted by Nick Haberling, CFP®, each episode breaks down important planning topics in a thoughtful, practical way for families, retirees, and business owners who want to make smarter decisions with their money.

title: Coffee With JM
author: JM
description:
JM spends a lot of time thinking, about serious things, unserious things, and mostly, things she doesn't even quite know how to define. to fulfill the urge to share it all, she made a podcast.


over a coffee, JM talks about whatever has been on her mind lately. relationships, culture, identity crises, or the silliest little observation on a random morning. you never really know what to expect, and that's part of the fun.


new episodes every thursday. JM will call. youu pick up. then we grab a coffee, pull up a chair, and hang out.

title: INDIECA WORLDS - The indie games podcast with an accent
author: Leonardo Tedeschi
description:
INDIECA WORLDS is the indie video game podcast where size doesn’t matter 🎮👾


A weekly trip through the world of independent games, exploring new releases, first impressions, weird discoveries, and the games currently destroying my controller, mouse, keyboard, and free time.


Every episode is a new Level: gaming news, upcoming indie titles, weekly releases, strange games from the depths of the web, and new tools and technologies for creating video games.


INDIECA WORLDS is part of INDIECA - Indie Creator Alliance, a creative network built to support, discover, and promote independent video games.


Follow or contact us:
https://linktr.ee/indieca


🤖 No pixels were harmed in the making of INDIECA WORLDS 🤖

title: Everything's Sales
author: Tara Shuler
description: What if the secret to getting what you want in life was simpler than you thought? On Everything is Sales, host Tara Shhuler breaks down the one skill that drives every outcome — the ability to sell your ideas, your value, and yourself.Tara is the founder of ConvoControl, a sales and communication consultancy built on one powerful truth: whoever controls the conversation controls the outcome. Through coaching, consulting, and her bestselling book Questions Close Deals, she has helped entrepreneurs, executives, and sales professionals transform the way they communicate — in the boardroom, in negotiations, and in life.Whether you're closing a multimillion-dollar deal or convincing your kid to eat their vegetables, this podcast will change the way you think about every conversation you have. Because everything — truly everything — is sales.

title: The Hook
author: Kyle "Trigger" Coroneos
description: Songwriting showcase that features established songwriters, and ANYONE else who wants to get up and perform a song. Totally unscripted, you never know who will show up and perform for a panel of experts.

title: The Keeper of Curious Books
author: Ink Press
description:
Join Laurie, owner of a small bookshop tucked away on a quiet street, as she shares calm, slow-paced stories filled with warmth, wonder, and the occasional touch of gentle magic. Each episode explores curious books, quiet moments, kind people, and the small wonders hidden within everyday life.


Laurie's Little Bookshop is a place to unwind after a busy day. Whether you’re settling in for the night, taking an afternoon nap, or simply looking for a moment of calm, these gentle stories are designed to help you slow down, relax, and let your mind wander somewhere peaceful.


So settle in. Take a breath. Find a comfortable spot.


There’s always another story waiting on the shelves.

title: Calma Sonrie
author: Valeria y Yuli
description:
Calma, sonríe es un podcast creado por dos mamás, odontólogas y amantes de la enseñanza, que comparten una filosofía de vida basada en la calma y en la sonrisa como forma de habitar el día a día.


Desde miradas y especialidades diferentes, compartimos conversaciones reales sobre salud, bienestar, emociones, maternidad, profesión y vida diaria.


Este espacio nace para odontólogos, pacientes y cualquier persona que quiera comprender la salud y la vida desde un lugar más consciente, calmado y sin miedo.


Aquí no solo hablamos de dientes, hablamos de personas.


Creemos que elegir la calma y sonreír siempre es una mejor opción.


Respira, escucha y sonríe.


Bienvenidos ✨

Vale y Yuli

From erosion control to essential driveway maintenance like chip seal and asphalt paving, we cover it all.

Join K103’s Whitney Thomas as she chats with country artists, local leaders, community members, and other fascinating guests. From music and memorable moments to hometown stories and plenty of laughs, every conversation feels like catching up with a friend.

title: AI Customer Success Brief
author: Jeff Kushmerek
description:
What operators are actually building with AI in customer success -- past the prototype and into production.
Each episode covers a real implementation: the problem, what got built, and how it got deployed. No vendor pitches, no roadmap theater. CS leaders, CCOs, and post-sales operators talking about what's running on their teams right now.

Our God Is So Amazing

title: Death and Breakfast
author: Your Favorite Georgia Funeral Director
description: Join the host of Death and Breakfast for a light-hearted mix of hilarious funeral home stories, behind-the-scenes insights, and honest conversations about death care. Blending humor with education, the podcast makes the funeral service industry approachable, informative, and surprisingly entertaining.

title: The Last Free Signal: San Angeles
author: Matthew Van Hove
description:
n the sprawling megacity of San Angeles, survival is never guaranteed.

The Last Free Signal is a gritty cyberpunk audio drama following Paz, a young runner caught between powerful forces, a spreading disease, and a city that chews up anyone who steps out of line.

As she navigates the neon-lit streets and dangerous underbelly of San Angeles, Paz begins to uncover truths that were never meant to see the light.

Dark, atmospheric, and full of tension, this is a story about rebellion, survival, and the voices that refuse to be silenced.

New episodes release regularly.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Phoenix Podfics
author: RaiderOfTheCreed
description:
Fan fiction reading - Dramione and others pairings eventually 😊
big shout out to AJKronhos for the logo design ❤️

title: The Alicia Branham Podcast
author: Alicia Branham
description:
The Alicia Branham Podcast is a show about leadership, sales, marketing, and growth — told honestly and from inside relationship-driven industries.
Hosted by Alicia Branham, this podcast dives into the real challenges facing manufacturers, manufacturer reps, distributors, and sales-driven organizations today. From outdated marketing and under-supported sales teams to leadership pressure and communication breakdowns, these are the conversations that aren’t happening loudly enough.
With more than 16 years of experience working alongside sales teams and leadership, Alicia brings practical insig

title: Tax Smart Retirement Podcast
author: Charles Winfrey
description:
Welcome to The Tax-Smart Retirement Podcast, the go-to podcast for anyone looking to take charge of their financial future. Hosted by Charles Winfrey of The Rollover Company, each episode dives into the essentials of 401(k) planning, lump-sum pensions, and IRAs with straightforward information and practical advice. Charles brings his experience as a financial advisor to the mic, making complex topics easy to understand for listeners at any stage of their financial journey.
Tune in to each new episode of The Tax-Smart Retirement Podcast for lively discussion packed with real-world tips and professional insights that will help you make smarter decisions and roll over your financial concerns into confidence!

title: All That is Known
author: Tess
description:
All That Is Known is a documentary podcast about one woman's lifelong search to understand where she came from.


What begins with a records request in 1981 becomes a decades-long pursuit through adoption files, court records, hospital archives, and family secrets—each discovery revealing new truths while raising new questions. 

 

At age sixty-eight, a chance encounter uncovers a record that appears to answer the largest remaining mystery, the identity of her father. Instead, as records often do, it opens an entirely new chapter of the search.


Season One is a serialized story and is best experienced in episode order, beginning with Episode 1: A Life Recorded.

title: The Human Story
author: The human
description:
Welcome to Think Deeper, a podcast that explores the fascinating questions behind human behavior, psychology, evolution, history, and philosophy. Every episode dives into the hidden reasons why we think, act, and live the way we do. If you're curious about the world and the human mind, you're in the right place.

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/the-human-story--7128116/support.

title: Keep Buggering On
author: Alexandria Quinn Love
description: Every episode starts with a shirt. KBO Standard Supply makes apparel with a phrase on it, and the phrase is never just a phrase — it's a door. Keep Buggering On walks through that door into whatever's actually on the other side: history nobody taught you right, a golf swing gone wrong, an ethics problem hiding in your last team meeting, a recipe that ended in minor property damage. Fully accurate. Genuinely funny. Never mean about it. We're not here to lecture you or take anyone down — just to report, with the receipts, exactly how absurd being human really is. We checked. It's real. You're not going to believe it.

title: Witness of History
author: Maged Atia
description:
History happened to real people.
These are their stories.
Witness of History is an immersive history podcast that tells the story of the ancient world through the eyes of those who lived it. Each episode is narrated by a witness: a stable boy, a sailor, a treasury clerk, ..etc.
If you love short stories you will love Witness of History. Expect battles, political intrigue, exploration, ambition, triumph, and tragedy.. told not by historians looking back, but by the people caught in the middle of it all.

title: Built in the Community with Chrissy
author: Chrissy Ransome - REACH
description: Built in the Community with Chrissy is a community storytelling platform highlighting local entrepreneurs, business owners, nonprofits, leaders, and changemakers making an impact. Through authentic conversations, we share the stories, challenges, and victories of the people helping build stronger communities.

title: Jesus wept
description: This happened when Jesus visited the tomb of Lazarus. Although Jesus knew He was about to raise Lazarus from the dead, He still wept when He saw the grief of Lazarus's sisters, Mary of Bethany and Martha of Bethany, and the sorrow of those around them.

title: Emboldened with Jessica
author: Jessica Jackson
description:
Emboldened with Jessica is a mental health and wellness podcast exploring belonging, trauma healing, adult friendships, burnout recovery, identity, and rebuilding community in an increasingly isolating world.
Hosted by licensed therapist and somatic practitioner Jessica Jackson, alongside producer and co-host Ariele, with cultural commentary and insight from Cultural Chatterist Kathleen Onyejekwe, the show blends psychology, nervous system science, lived experience, and cultural analysis to explore how we navigate relationships, healing, visibility, and self-trust in complex times.
Each episode explores topics like:
• the loneliness epidemic and rebuilding community
• somatic therapy and nervous system regulation
• adult friendships and social exhaustion
• trauma, shame, and healing
• neurodivergence and masking
• burnout and high-achiever recovery
• decolonizing mental health
• relationships, boundaries, and belonging
• identity shifts, embodiment, and personal growth
Rather than offering surface-level self-help, Emboldened with Jessica examines the deeper cultural, relational, and psychological forces shaping how we live, connect, and understand ourselves. 
Conversations are thoughtful, honest, reflective, and often humorous — inviting listeners to move away from comparison and performance and toward embodied, sustainable ways of living.
This podcast is not therapy and is not a substitute for working with a licensed mental health professional. It is a space for reflection, learning, emotional wellness, and collective care.
At its heart, Emboldened with Jessica is an invitation to embrace your worth, reconnect with yourself, and build a life that feels more honest, grounded, and fully your own.
A space where complexity is welcome, curiosity is encouraged, and your worth is not up for debate.

title: Horizonte Aberto
author: Amine
description: I would prefer not to.

title: Driven to Succeed
author: driventosucceed
description: *Driven to Succeed* is a podcast that delves into the authentic stories of individuals who have achieved success in various facets of life. Through candid conversations, the show aims to explore themes of truth, vulnerability, and commonality, offering a platform for shared experiences and valuable insights. Join host Adam Fetterman as he uncovers the driving forces behind success and the real stories behind the facade.

title: AAMS On Air
author: Association of Air Medical Services
description: AAMS On Air is the official podcast of the Association of Air Medical Services (AAMS), spotlighting the voices, ideas, and innovations advancing air medical and critical care transport. Each episode features expert insights across four lenses: policy and advocacy, safety and operations, education and best practices, and emerging leaders. Designed for all professionals across air medical and critical care transport programs, AAMS On Air delivers timely conversations, expert interviews, and practical knowledge to keep the community informed and connected.

title: Beyond the Circuit: Where Tomorrow Whisp
author: Smith
description:
Beyond the Circuit is not another technology podcast about gadgets, apps, or breaking news. It explores the hidden technologies shaping humanity before they become mainstream. From artificial intelligence that predicts human behavior to bioengineered materials, quantum communication, digital consciousness, and futuristic cities, each episode uncovers the innovations quietly transforming our future.

This podcast is designed for curious minds who want to understand what happens before technology becomes a trend. Every episode combines storytelling, research, philosophy, and future predictions to reveal the unseen side of innovation.

title: The Sustainability Magazine Podcast
author: Sustainability Magazine
description: It's a podcast about sustainability

title: My First Year: The Anthology
author: Sophia Media
description:
My First Year: The Anthology is an original Sophia Media podcast series that explores the human experience through deeply personal and reflective conversations about the first year of many major life transitions. 


Whether you’re becoming a parent, starting college or a new profession, moving to a new country, entering or leaving a major relationship, life transitions can feel isolating and disorienting. Each  “My First Year” show provides an intimate safe space for our guests and listeners alike to pause and reflect on these pivotal moments. Through vulnerable, embodied conversations, guests don’t just describe what happened – they sit with their emotions, key memories, and articulate the shifts in how they see themselves and the world. 


My First Year exists to heal through embodied story-telling. When we feel a visceral connection in our bodies to someone else’s story, we realize we’re not alone. That recognition – that our experiences echo in others – is where healing and growth begin. 


Hosted by Sophia, Gabbie Watts, Rosabelle Heine, Samira Tazari, Adoley Ammah-Tagoe

Produced by Gabbie Watts, Rosabelle Heine, and Samira Tazari

Creative Direction by Shareen Anderson

title: IN CASE OF EMERGENCY
description: When the news, current events, and other stuff happens, it's time to take action immediately.

title: The contrastbyhanafuda's Podcast
author: contrastbyhanafuda
description: Two dynamic duos D and Em from Vancouver, BC, Canada, have different views. One day they may agree on something... but who knows.

title: Someone Has To Be Brave
author: Andrea Wright
description:
Someone Has To Be Brave; A Journey Through Resilience and Healing
Welcome to "Someone Has To Be Brave," a podcast where we delve into the raw, unfiltered journey of resilience, survival, and healing. Based on powerful and deeply personal book entries, this podcast explores the multifaceted experiences of a Black woman, vet, mom and neurodivergent navigating the complexities of life, love, and self-discovery.
Join us as we uncover the harrowing and inspiring stories of overcoming toxic relationships, battling societal and familial expectations, and finding strength in the face of adversity.

title: The Gross Score
author: Robbie Gross
description: This podcast will feature a faith based outdoor conversation. If you're tuning into the show, you can expect The Gross Score to deliver in-depth analysis on hunting strategies and faith stories that are sure to get you ready for a Sunday service or a afternoon in the field. Welcome to the Gross Score, Where faith meets the field and truth is revealed.

title: Gathering Of Tribes: Come Follow Me
author: Chuck Kaye & Rachel Crouse
description:
Gathering of Tribes is a non-profit organization dedicated to strengthening faithful Latter-day Saints and promoting Indigenous voices. 


On this show, we sit down with fellow Indigenous Latter-day Saints and other interesting guests to discuss the weekly scripture reading in the “Come Follow Me” curriculum produced by the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints.

title: Liminal Conversations with Anna Ewa Parkman
author: Anna Parkman
description: Exploring consciousness, spiritual awakening, anxiety healing, life purpose, relationships, energy, and the journey of the soul. These conversations are designed to help you understand yourself, transform your life, and remember who you truly are.

title: Retirement Institute
author: The Retirement Institute
description:
Selected episodes from the popular radio show The Retirement Institute Show. 

Contact: 888.330.9920  |   retirementinstituteradio.com

title: Tale of the Witch Doll: A Penny Parker Mystery
description: In "Tale of the Witch Doll," the first book of the Penny Parker Mystery Series, young Penny Parker embarks on an exciting adventure filled with mystery and intrigue. When Penny discovers a doll rumored to be cursed, she can't resist the urge to investigate its dark past. Set against a backdrop of suspense and friendship, this story showcases the power of curiosity and bravery. As Penny digs deeper, she encounters unexpected twists and turns that challenge her cleverness and determination. This captivating tale, penned by the talented Mildred A. Wirt Benson, known for creating the beloved Nancy Drew character, invites listeners to join Penny as she uncovers secrets and solves puzzles. Perfect for children and families, this audiobook sparks imagination and encourages young listeners to embrace their inner detective. Get ready for a thrilling ride filled with unexpected surprises, as Penny Parker proves that sometimes the greatest mysteries are hidden in plain sight.

title: Apple Is Not Banana
author: Lingling
description: Funny, real, brave, a little unpredictable, makes you see your own life differently. Not a therapist. Just someone who's lived, reflected, and isn't afraid to say the real thing out loud.

title: Daily Faith with Philip Cameron
author: Daily Faith With Philip Cameron
description: Faith-filled conversations with pastors, leaders, and everyday believers, hosted by Philip Cameron.

title: What Holds
author: Carla Royal
description: What Holds is a podcast about the work that holds what founders and leaders are building. Each conversation looks beneath performance, self-sufficiency, and success into what people carry, what it costs, and what becomes possible when they are no longer carrying it alone.

title: LiveAI by Trace3
author: Trace3
description:
ChatGPT and GenAl tools have hit the mainstream. The chatter is everywhere, had by almost everyone.

At Trace3, we feel the discussion has been more philosophical than practical. We know enterprise technologists are using real Al to create efficiencies that result in real business outcomes. So we created a show to talk to real practitioners, about real-world, active projects that are leveraging this exciting new technical advantage.


Welcome to LiveAl!

title: Beyond the Informer
author: Beyond The Informer
description: Beyond the Informer is a paranormal podcast exploring the unexplained, the mysterious, and the stories that defy logic. From ghost encounters and UFO sightings to cryptids, conspiracies, and real-life experiences, each episode dives deep into the unknown. If you're fascinated by what lies beyond reality, this is the podcast that takes you there.

title: Soul or Rational Psychology
description: Swedenborg, Emanuel, 1688-1772, was born in Stockholm, Sweden and died in London, England. He was a voluminous writer of scientific treatises as well as prophetic works such as Archana Caelestia and The Divine Providence. He said he had encountered supranational agencies and communicated with angels. This is a recording of the 1849 translation of his 1743 book The Soul or Rational Psychology (Regnum Animale, Pars Septima De Anima) Latin. He took his cue from Aristotle's De Anima. A few quotes It has been shown above that the harmonies themselves are innate with us, or that we perceive them without a teacher; as the sweetnesses of taste and smell, the symmetries of sound, the excellencies and beauties of nature; in a word, the very order of things or the harmony of modes, forces, substances, and forms. Thence also we may perceive the very truths of things, for these correspond to the order itself in nature; and this is the reason why order is called "the transcendental truth." In order that the human intellect may exist, it is necessary that the truths themselves be variegated and as it were modified by things mendacious, or true things with false, good with evil. There are as many worlds as there are terrestrial societies, and there are as many heavens as there are celestial societies. The love of society is both natural and acquired, for to live alone or to live without society is not to live, for whatever is one's own is not known as one's own except from others, or relatively. When we strive for and desire what we love, and yet impossibilities interfere with our attaining our end, we call this state of desire hope, and it seems to be in the will viewed as an endeavour which these obstacles are preventing from coming forth into act and motion. Thus hope is not an affection of the mind, but of its will. For the will always endeavours to act, but so long as it is resisted does not act. Meanwhile it is affected by a certain hope, so that it remains balanced between action and inaction. Despair exists when we cut off hope; then also when, in the end itself, love and ambition, that is, the life and ardour of the mind, collapse and are as it were extinguished. There is a liberty of thinking and a liberty of acting; and in the middle between these two there is as it were the liberty of choosing arbitrandt, in which properly free will consists; and that our mind is not capable of ruling whether the objects of the senses and their exciting influences, both from the body and the world, shall flow in or not, but it is capable of choosing whether these sensations and excitements shall flow out and be determined into act." This third edition of the 1849 translation was published in 1914. Johann Friedrich Immanuel Tafel, 1796-1863 devoted himself to translating Swedenborg into German, and Frank Sewall, 1837-1915 into English. Sewall included a long introduction and three appendixes to Swedenborg's work. The introduction is included in this reading but the appendixes have been omitted.

title: Selected Essays of Michel de Montaigne
description: Michel de Montaigne was a contemporary of Shakespeare who, after having played his part in France's terrible wars of religion, retired to his estates and decided to write about what he knew best--himself. And so in his likes and dislikes, his reflections on history, his loves and his travels, he brought to life, not just one man, but all human experience. "What do I know?" he asked.
